title: eriogonum zionis
intro: Eriogonum zionis, also known as Zion buckwheat, is a rare and endangered wildflower native to southwestern Utah, USA. It is a shrubby perennial with small, white or pink flowers and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing, due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Eriogonum zionis can be made into a tea or tincture, although further research is needed to confirm its safety and efficacy for human consumption.
